items.body: July brings the Claret Jug back to Royal Birkdale — towering dunes, firm links, and golf's oldest major on the Merseyside coast. Scheffler defends after missing the cut at the Scottish Open, while Fleetwood plays a hometown Open a few miles from home.

There's always some random Europoors up there. Look for names like Jordan Smith, Eugenio Chacarra, and Tom fuckin McKibben to contend up until the back nine on Sunday. The Nose's sleeper pick of the week is Victor Perez. 3 straight top 10's on the DP tour and like all LIV players, he's looking for the exit. Most of all, though, I just hope some nerd like Bryan Harman doesn't win it.

items.label: Scottie Scheffler (+550 to +750):
items.body: Defending Open champion and clear favorite, hunting a repeat after missing the cut at the Scottish Open and getting an early first look at Birkdale.

items.label: Rory McIlroy (+650 to +900):
items.body: 2014 Open champion who finished T4 the last time The Open was at Royal Birkdale in 2017.

items.label: Jon Rahm (+1550 to +2000):
items.body: Major champion with the power and links temperament to contend when Birkdale gets firm and windy.

items.label: Tommy Fleetwood (+1400 to +2100):
items.body: Southport native playing a true hometown Open at Royal Birkdale, where he finished T27 in 2017.

items.label: Matt Fitzpatrick (+1500 to +2100):
items.body: English major winner and one of the steadiest wind players near the top of the board.

items.label: Xander Schauffele (+2000 to +2200):
items.body: 2024 Open champion returning to links golf with the ball-striking to handle a par-70 dune course.

items.label: Ludvig Aberg (+2200 to +3100):
items.body: Elite iron player with the length and control that fit an accuracy-first Birkdale setup.

items.label: Cameron Young (+2800 to +3000):
items.body: Powerful ball-striker who profiles well on firm links when the wind asks for flight control.

items.label: Chris Gotterup (+2800 to +3300):
items.body: Riding hot form into the year's final major after winning the John Deere as the defending Scottish Open champion.

items.label: Collin Morikawa (+2700 to +3100):
items.body: 2021 Open champion whose precision iron play fits a Birkdale test that rewards fairways and greens.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: Royal Birkdale Golf Club in Southport, Merseyside, England, a dune links on the Lancashire coast.

items.label: Established:
items.body: The Open Championship dates to 1860; Royal Birkdale first hosted in 1954 and this is its 11th Open.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Scottie Scheffler won the 2025 Open at Royal Portrush by four shots, his first Claret Jug.

items.label: Tradition:
items.body: Golf's oldest major crowns the Champion Golfer of the Year with the Claret Jug; Jordan Spieth won the last Birkdale Open in 2017.

items.label: Course:
items.body: Royal Birkdale Golf Club, Southport, England.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: July 16 through July 19, 2026.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: TBA ($17 million in 2025).

items.label: Format:
items.body: 156-player field, 72-hole stroke play with a cut after 36 holes.

items.label: Course Profile:
items.body: A 7,223-yard par-70 links routed through sand dunes, with firm fairways, heavy rough, and coastal wind that rewards accuracy over raw length.

items.label: Coverage:
items.body: Thursday and Friday early on Peacock (1:30-4 a.m. ET), then USA Network (4 a.m.-3:30 p.m. ET); weekend early on USA Network, main broadcast on NBC/Peacock (7 a.m. ET onward), with Live From The Open on Golf Channel.

items.label: Event Window:
items.body: Competition runs July 16 through July 19, 2026.
